name: Botox for Forehead Lines
description: Botulinum toxin type A injection to smooth horizontal forehead lines and prevent deepening of wrinkles. One of the most popular non-surgical aesthetic treatments worldwide.
procedure: After cleansing the area, small amounts of Botox are injected into specific muscles using a fine needle. Typically 10-30 units depending on muscle strength and desired effect. The procedure takes about 10-15 minutes.
downtime: Minimal. Avoid exercise for 24 hours, no lying down for 4 hours post-treatment.
resultsTimeline: Initial effects in 3-5 days, full results at 10-14 days
longevity: 3-4 months on average, may last longer with regular treatments
aftercare: Do not lie down for 4 hours after treatment
Avoid strenuous exercise for 24 hours
Do not rub or massage the treated area
Avoid alcohol for 24 hours
Avoid extreme heat (saunas, hot tubs) for 48 hours
drCostiApproach: I prefer a natural, conservative approach to forehead Botox. The goal is to soften lines while maintaining natural expression and movement. I customize the dosage based on each patient's muscle strength and aesthetic goals.

name: Botox for Crow's Feet
description: Treatment of fine lines around the eyes (lateral canthal lines) using botulinum toxin. These dynamic wrinkles appear when smiling or squinting.
procedure: 2-3 small injections per side around the outer corner of each eye. Uses 8-16 units per side. Quick and well-tolerated procedure.
downtime: None. Can return to normal activities immediately.
resultsTimeline: Visible improvement in 3-5 days, full effect at 2 weeks
aftercare: Avoid rubbing the eye area
No heavy exercise for 24 hours
Wear sunglasses to protect the area
drCostiApproach: I focus on maintaining a natural smile while reducing the depth of crow's feet. Overtreating this area can create an unnatural appearance, so precision is key.

name: Lip Filler (Hyaluronic Acid)
description: Enhancement of lip volume, shape, and definition using hyaluronic acid-based dermal fillers. Can address thin lips, asymmetry, and loss of volume due to aging.
procedure: After applying topical numbing cream, hyaluronic acid filler is carefully injected using a fine needle or cannula. Typically 0.5-1ml per session. Multiple techniques used based on desired outcome.
downtime: Swelling and bruising for 3-7 days. Social downtime 2-3 days.
resultsTimeline: Immediate volume, final shape at 2 weeks after swelling resolves
longevity: 6-12 months depending on product and metabolism
aftercare: Apply ice to reduce swelling
Avoid hot beverages for 24 hours
No kissing or lip pressure for 48 hours
Avoid strenuous exercise for 48 hours
Stay hydrated
drCostiApproach: My philosophy for lips is enhancement, not transformation. I aim for natural-looking results that complement the patient's facial proportions. I use the Russian lip technique for a defined, elegant shape when appropriate.

name: PRP Hair Restoration
description: Platelet-Rich Plasma therapy for hair loss. Uses the patient's own blood platelets to stimulate hair follicles, promote new hair growth, and slow hair thinning.
procedure: Blood is drawn, centrifuged to concentrate platelets, then the PRP is injected into the scalp at the level of hair follicles. Typically 3-4 sessions initially, then maintenance every 6-12 months.
downtime: Minimal. Mild scalp tenderness for 1-2 days.
resultsTimeline: Initial improvement at 3-6 months, optimal results after 3 sessions
longevity: 6-12 months per session, ongoing maintenance recommended
aftercare: Avoid washing hair for 24 hours
No harsh chemicals or styling for 48 hours
Avoid strenuous exercise for 24 hours
Avoid sun exposure to scalp for 48 hours
drCostiApproach: PRP is an excellent option for early-stage hair loss. I combine it with a comprehensive approach including topical treatments and lifestyle modifications for best results.

name: Medium-Depth Chemical Peel
description: Professional chemical exfoliation using TCA (trichloroacetic acid) to address sun damage, fine lines, acne scarring, and hyperpigmentation. Penetrates to the upper dermis for significant skin renewal.
contraindications: very dark skin tones (higher risk of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation)
procedure: Skin is cleansed and degreased. TCA solution (15-35%) is applied in controlled layers. A frosting pattern indicates depth of penetration. Neutralization may or may not be needed depending on the acid.
downtime: 5-7 days of peeling. Redness may persist 1-2 weeks.
resultsTimeline: New skin emerges in 7-10 days, continued improvement over 4-6 weeks
longevity: Results last 6-12 months with proper skincare
aftercare: Keep skin moisturized at all times
Absolutely no sun exposure during healing
Use SPF 50+ daily for at least 3 months
Do not pick or peel the skin
Gentle cleanser only during healing
drCostiApproach: I select the peel depth and concentration based on each patient's skin type, concerns, and downtime tolerance. For patients with Middle Eastern skin, I'm particularly careful with concentration to avoid post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation.
